title: [$250] P2P - Transaction details and legal disclosures are missing in the payment email

labels.name: Help Wanted

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.70-1 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://test-management.browserstack.com/projects/2219752/folder/13176725/test-cases/41237082 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** N/A **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Regression TC Execution **Device used:** MacBook Air 15.7.4 Chrome **App Component:** Notifications ## Action Performed: Preconditions: User A and User B have wallet enabled and positive balance User B is logged out User B's email can be accessed 1. As user A, navigate to the chat with User B 2. Send a message to user B 3. Click on the + button in the compose box, select "Pay User B" 4. Enter the amount, select "Pay with Wallet" 5. Open the emails in User B's Inbox Verify the other email is be a receipt that has a IOU preview at the top and should also have a footer that includes more detailed transaction information and a list of legal disclosures ## Expected Result: The email with the subject "User A paid User B" includes more detailed transaction information and a list of legal disclosures The email with the subject "Chat with User A" User A's payment preview is not shown as an expense, sent by User B ## Actual Result: The email with the subject "User A paid User B" doesn't include detailed transaction information and a list of legal disclosures The email with the subject "Chat with User A" User A's payment preview is shown as an expense, sent by User B In Prod: 1st issue is the same, 2nd issue - "Chat" email is not received ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ed7f0b27-ed6b-4ec9-879d-649e14884cef [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

title: [$250] Always show Update credentials for Sage Intacct connections

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
Currently, the "Enter credentials" / "Update credentials" option in the accounting connection overflow menu is only shown when there's been a sync error due to incorrect credentials. This is controlled by the condition at [`PolicyAccountingPage.tsx:113`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/b4103e0062b3f68c9603257c024baa65f669e57b/src/pages/workspace/accounting/PolicyAccountingPage.tsx#L113): ```typescript const shouldShowEnterCredentials = connectedIntegration && !!synchronizationError && isAuthenticationError(policy, connectedIntegration); ``` This requires `lastSync.isAuthenticationError === true` ([`connections/index.ts:263`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/b4103e0062b3f68c9603257c024baa65f669e57b/src/libs/actions/connections/index.ts#L263)), meaning users can only update credentials reactively after they break — not proactively. While this makes sense for OAuth-based connections (e.g., QuickBooks, Xero) and NetSuite tokens, Sage Intacct uses a Web Services user with a company ID, user ID, and password. Users should be able to proactively update these credentials (e.g., during a planned password rotation) without waiting for a sync failure. ## Proposed Change For Sage Intacct connections, always show the "Update credentials" option in the overflow menu regardless of whether there's been an authentication error. The "Sync Now" option should still be available alongside it. **Slack thread:** https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C03U7DCU4/p1778544534188309
